question.label: Cross-org decisions
question.name: Cross-org decision area — which decisions do you want your name attached to?
question.description: Turns his stated aspiration into a concrete picture of the scope and decisions he wants to own.

answer: I also need clearer sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set.

assessment.note: Deficiency-as-request: he names missing sponsorship in priority-setting rooms as a strong growth constraint; also implies a clarity issue off-signature.

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Sponsorship need
new_queue.name: What kind of sponsorship would actually change the way your work lands here?

new_queue.label: Next two weeks
new_queue.name: Given what we've talked through, what would you want to focus on first over the next two weeks?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 6)

history.answer_excerpt: Sometimes I avoid high-ambiguity forums because I do not want to sound underprepared.
I think I need to own a cross-org decision area, not just deliver strong design outcomes.
I am still motivated, but the promotion outcome made me more careful about where I spend political capital.

I can lead major initiatives, but I do not always translate that into visible strategic influence.
I get positive feedback on craft and collaboration, but less clarity on what separates me at the next level.
